evidence_quotes.quote: disabling games that have already been sold to the consumer.

paragraphs.text: Yours sincerely, I am writing to comment on two issues: The first is to call for the existing terms of the Digital Fair Act to include a new one protecting consumers from the increasingly widespread practice in video game companies of disabling games that have already been sold to the consumer. This means that these companies should not be allowed to cut off access to games that have already been paid in full without giving them some sort of exit so that consumers can continue to enjoy them. This is because if these harmful actions are not stopped, there will be a moment when one will pay for any game and, if the purchase comes a little late when the game is launched, the third day of opening the game will no longer have access to it. A situation that I find deplorable and entrist, which, under this premise, comes to a day when playing jewellery of the past is impossible, that the games that one has to disappear from the personal library should not be returned and there is nothing to prevent it. The second is the actions of Visa and Mastercard colleagues, who have used their monopoly as payment transaction entities to force video game platforms to eliminate games globally in accordance with the guidelines of an Australian political organisation. I consider this to be an infringement of the rights to personal liberty in the case of games which were not in breach of the law and which, moreover, have been withdrawn exclusively to complain about what the political party regards as fit or not to the public on the basis of his or her personal beliefs. I would add that I am neither part of the public nor interested in the games that have been removed, but I consider that what happened cannot be allowed, failing which everything will end up in a global censorship based on what a group in one part of it creates is good or not without there being a legal basis underpinning those beliefs for the rest. That is why I am writing all of this to bring my granite of sand and see if something can be done in this respect. Thank you very much for reading my petition, Atentently, a European citizen
